findings.evidence: `rg -n "orbitQuotient.*iff|mk.*iff|proj.*iff|mk.*eq|eq.*mk" TauCeti/AlgebraicTopology/UniversalCover/Deck/Quotient.lean` only finds the private transport iff at line 78, not a regular quotient equality characterization.
findings.fix: Add a public iff lemma, preferably simp-normalized, e.g. `orbitQuotient_mk_eq_mk_iff (hreg : IsRegular p) : (Quotient.mk'' e = Quotient.mk'' e') ↔ p e = p e'`.
findings.issue: `orbitQuotientEquivBase` does not provide the characteristic regular-quotient equality lemma `Quotient.mk'' e = Quotient.mk'' e' ↔ p e = p e'`, so users must reconstruct the main quotient/fibre identification from the equivalence.

findings.evidence: `smul_eq_apply` at line 93 and fibre inverse evaluation at line 85 are simp lemmas; `inv_smul_eq_symm_apply` at line 97 is the same evaluation API but lacks the annotation.
findings.fix: Add `@[simp]` to `inv_smul_eq_symm_apply` so inverse deck actions reduce to the underlying inverse homeomorphism automatically.
findings.issue: `inv_smul_eq_symm_apply` is a normal-form lemma for evaluating inverse deck actions but is not tagged `@[simp]`, unlike the parallel evaluation lemmas.

summary: The main quotient/base equivalence is exposed with representative simp lemmas, but the public API still misses the quotient equality characterization and one new normal-form lemma is not available to `simp`.
